claim.hypotheses.explanation: Includes H1 verbatim matching the reference hypothesis, but also adds H2 and H3 which are not part of the referenced claim.

claim.hypotheses_location.explanation: Correctly identifies H1 location (p. 350, The Effect of Branding on Attitude Functions) but adds locations for H2 and H3 not present in the reference.

claim.statement.explanation: Conveys the paper’s main claim via the title and matches the core idea, but the reference statement is a specific result excerpt from Study 1B with statistics.

claim.statement_location.explanation: Points to title/abstract and general discussion, whereas the reference locates the statement in Study 1B on p. 351.

data.source.explanation: Both describe an author-run experimental survey/lab with students; phrasing differs (lab vs survey).

data.wave_or_subset.explanation: Reference indicates NA; extracted specifies multiple studies, which does not match the NA designation.

data.sample_size.explanation: Reference focuses on N=206 (Study 1B); extracted lists multiple study Ns instead of the single reference value.

data.notes.explanation: Reference provides no notes; extracted adds details not present in the reference.

method.description.explanation: Accurately describes RCT with branded vs unbranded evaluations and mixed ANOVA; aligns with reference.

method.steps.explanation: Covers the procedural flow and aligns with reference steps, with additional details beyond the referenced outline.

method.models.explanation: Includes the referenced mixed ANOVA but also lists additional analyses not in the reference.

method.outcome_variable.explanation: Includes the Benefit–Symbolism Difference Score (reference) along with other outcomes not specified in the reference.

method.independent_variables.explanation: Includes Branding (reference) plus additional predictors from other studies.

method.control_variables.explanation: Reference indicates NA; extracted lists procedural controls not specified in the reference.

results.summary.explanation: Accurately summarizes that branding shifts attitude functions and attenuates matching advantages, consistent with the reference.
